photos.title: Quiet Street in Gamla Stan
photos.alt: A quiet cobblestone street in Gamla Stan, Stockholm, lined with historic buildings, hanging signs, street lamps, flowers, and Swedish flags.
photos.caption: A quiet street scene in Gamla Stan, where cobblestones, historic façades, and Swedish flags create a calm and timeless atmosphere.

photos.title: Crowded Street in Gamla Stan
photos.alt: A crowded street in Gamla Stan, Stockholm, with people walking between historic buildings, shop signs, restaurants, and narrow old-town façades.
photos.caption: A busy summer street in Gamla Stan, where historic façades, restaurant signs, and passing crowds shape the rhythm of Stockholm’s old town.

photos.title: Stockholm City Hall
photos.alt: Stockholm City Hall seen from across the water, with its tall brick tower rising under a cloudy sky.
photos.caption: Stockholm City Hall stands quietly by the water, its brick tower framed by clouds and the soft rhythm of the city.

photos.title: Looking Toward Riddarholmen
photos.alt: A view from Klara Mälarstrand in Stockholm looking across the water toward Riddarholmen, with an elderly man sitting alone by the waterfront in the foreground.
photos.caption: From Klara Mälarstrand, an elderly man sits quietly by the water, facing Riddarholmen and the layered skyline of Stockholm.

photos.title: View Toward Söder Mälarstrand
photos.alt: A waterfront view from Södra Riddarholmshamnen in Stockholm, looking across the water toward Söder Mälarstrand and Mariahissen, with historic buildings rising above the shoreline.
photos.caption: From Södra Riddarholmshamnen, the view opens across the water toward Söder Mälarstrand, where Mariahissen and the hillside buildings shape Stockholm’s layered skyline.

photos.title: Old Façade in Gamla Stan
photos.alt: An upward view of an old building façade in Gamla Stan, Stockholm, with a pale gabled wall, small windows, and warm-toned historic architecture below.
photos.caption: An old façade in Gamla Stan rises into the soft sky, revealing the quiet geometry and aged surfaces of Stockholm’s historic core.

photos.title: Yttre Borggården and Stockholm Cathedral
photos.alt: A view from Yttre Borggården in Gamla Stan, Stockholm, with people standing in the courtyard and the tower of Stockholm Cathedral visible beyond the palace buildings.
photos.caption: From Yttre Borggården, the tower of Stockholm Cathedral rises beyond the palace courtyard, connecting the open square with the old city skyline.

photos.title: View Toward the German Church
photos.alt: A cobblestone street in Gamla Stan, Stockholm, leading toward the tower of the German Church between historic buildings.
photos.caption: A quiet view through Gamla Stan, where the tower of the German Church rises between old façades and cobblestone streets.

photos.title: Stone Ship at Anundshög
photos.alt: A stone ship formation at Anundshög in Västerås, Sweden, surrounded by grassy burial mounds, leafless trees, and open Nordic landscape.
photos.caption: A stone ship at Anundshög, standing quietly among burial mounds and open fields near Västerås.

photos.title: Anundshög Runestone, Vs 13
photos.alt: The Anundshög Runestone, known as Vs 13, standing at Anundshög in Västerås, Sweden, with a runic inscription commemorating Heden, son of Folkvid and brother of Anund.
photos.caption: The Anundshög Runestone, Vs 13, bears the inscription: “Folkvid raised all these stones in memory of his son Heden, Anund’s brother. Vred carved the runes.”

photos.title: Round Bastion at Landskrona Slott
photos.alt: A round red brick bastion at Landskrona Slott in Sweden, seen beside the water with historic castle buildings and a cloudy sky in the background.
photos.caption: A round bastion at Landskrona Slott, where red brick walls, water, and spring light meet in a quiet historic setting.

photos.title: Landskrona Slott Across the Moat
photos.alt: A wide view of Landskrona Slott in Sweden, with red brick castle buildings, a wooden bridge, the surrounding moat, and a bright cloudy sky.
photos.caption: Landskrona Slott stands across the moat, its red brick walls and wooden bridge reflected in the calm water.

photos.title: Courtyard of Landskrona Slott
photos.alt: The inner courtyard of Landskrona Slott in Sweden, with pink and red brick historic buildings, green-framed windows, and a single person standing near the center.
photos.caption: Inside the courtyard of Landskrona Slott, weathered brick walls, muted pink façades, and open space create a quiet architectural rhythm.

photos.title: Passage Through Landskrona Slott
photos.alt: An arched passage inside Landskrona Slott, with red brick walls, white archways, and two people walking across the bridge in the distance.
photos.caption: A passage through Landskrona Slott frames the bridge beyond, turning brick, shadow, and daylight into a quiet architectural sequence.

photos.title: Rooflines of Landskrona Slott
photos.alt: A close architectural view of Landskrona Slott, showing red brick walls, tiled rooflines, a round tower roof, and blue sky with clouds above.
photos.caption: Red brick, tiled roofs, and soft clouds define the quiet architectural character of Landskrona Slott.

photos.title: Ales Stenar Under a Grey Sky
photos.alt: Ales Stenar stone ship in Kåseberga, Sweden, with large standing stones arranged in a ship formation under a grey cloudy sky.
photos.caption: At Ales Stenar, standing stones form a quiet ship-shaped monument above the coast, held between open ground and a heavy Nordic sky.

photos.title: Standing Stone at Ales Stenar
photos.alt: A close view of a large standing stone at Ales Stenar in Kåseberga, Sweden, with other stones forming a ship-shaped monument under a grey cloudy sky.
photos.caption: A single standing stone anchors the view at Ales Stenar, surrounded by the wider stone ship and the muted openness of the Skåne coast.

photos.title: Ales Stenar on the Horizon
photos.alt: A distant view of Ales Stenar in Kåseberga, Sweden, with standing stones silhouetted along the horizon under a wide grey sky and visitors walking nearby.
photos.caption: Ales Stenar stretches along the horizon, its standing stones silhouetted beneath a vast grey sky above the Skåne coast.

photos.title: Church of Old Uppsala
photos.alt: The Church of Old Uppsala in Sweden, seen on a grassy hill surrounded by trees, with stone walls, a red brick gable, and a blue sky above.
photos.caption: The Church of Old Uppsala rises quietly above the grass, framed by trees, spring light, and the deep historical landscape of Gamla Uppsala.

photos.title: Front of the Church of Old Uppsala
photos.alt: The front façade of the Church of Old Uppsala in Sweden, showing a tall medieval stone church with a red brick entrance, benches, green grass, and a blue sky with clouds.
photos.caption: The front of the Church of Old Uppsala stands in clear spring light, its stone façade framed by grass, benches, and a wide blue sky.

photos.title: Uppsala Church Bell Tower
photos.alt: The wooden bell tower beside the Church of Old Uppsala in Sweden, standing among trees, gravestones, grass, and a bright spring sky.
photos.caption: Beside the Church of Old Uppsala, the wooden bell tower stands quietly among trees and gravestones, adding a warm historic presence to the churchyard.

photos.title: Runestone U 978 at Old Uppsala Church
photos.alt: Runestone U 978 built into the wall of the Church of Old Uppsala in Sweden, showing a carved cross, runic inscription, and weathered stone surface.
photos.caption: Runestone U 978, set into the wall of the Church of Old Uppsala, carries the inscription: “Sigvid, the England traveller, raised this stone in memory of Vidjärv, his father …”

photos.title: Lövånger Church
photos.alt: Lövånger Church in Västerbotten, Sweden, with a white church building, wooden bell tower, road signs, patches of snow, and a clear blue sky.
photos.caption: Lövånger Church stands in clear spring light, its white walls and wooden bell tower set against snow, road signs, and the quiet landscape of Västerbotten.

photos.title: Lövånger Church in Spring Light
photos.alt: Lövånger Church in Västerbotten, Sweden, seen from the side with white walls, a dark roof, patches of snow, bare trees, and a clear blue spring sky.
photos.caption: Lövånger Church stands in sharp spring light, its white walls and dark roof contrasting with melting snow and the clear northern sky.

photos.title: Lövånger Kyrkstad
photos.alt: Red wooden church town cottages at Lövånger Kyrkstad in Västerbotten, Sweden, standing in spring snow under a clear blue sky.
photos.caption: Red wooden cottages line the snowy path at Lövånger Kyrkstad, preserving the quiet rhythm of a historic Swedish church town.

photos.title: Cottage at Lövånger Kyrkstad
photos.alt: A red wooden cottage at Lövånger Kyrkstad in Västerbotten, Sweden, standing on rocky ground with a yellow door, wooden steps, bare trees, and a clear blue sky.
photos.caption: A small red cottage at Lövånger Kyrkstad stands on the rock, its yellow door and weathered steps preserving the quiet character of the historic church town.

photos.title: Weathered Window at Lövånger Kyrkstad
photos.alt: A close view of a weathered yellow wooden window shutter set into a red wooden building at Lövånger Kyrkstad in Västerbotten, Sweden.
photos.caption: A weathered yellow shutter set into red timber, preserving the texture, age, and quiet character of Lövånger Kyrkstad.

photos.title: Glimmingehus Across the Moat
photos.theme: Castle, Medieval Architecture, Moat, Stone Keep
photos.alt: The tall stone keep of Glimmingehus rises beside a white-walled manor house, reflected in the surrounding moat under an overcast spring sky, with a wooden footbridge crossing the water to the right.
photos.caption: Scandinavia's best-preserved medieval stronghold, mirrored in its moat.

photos.title: Cobbled Courtyard at Glimmingehus
photos.alt: The empty cobblestone courtyard of Glimmingehus, enclosed by ochre-yellow outbuildings with red tile roofs, with a wooden replica pillory standing at its centre under an overcast spring sky.
photos.caption: Inside the walls at Glimmingehus, a cobbled courtyard with a wooden pillory at its heart.

photos.title: Saltängsbron Runestone (Vs 9)
photos.theme: Runestone, Viking Age, Ancient Inscription, Nordic Heritage
photos.alt: A weathered grey runestone stands upright in a green late-summer meadow, its looping runic band and serpent ornament picked out in red, with birch trees and open Västmanland fields behind it.
photos.caption: Raised by Gísl in memory of his son Ásl, who died in England — its runes still traced in red across the granite.

photos.title: Runestone Vs 10, Västerås
photos.theme: Runestone, Viking Age, Zoomorphic Ornament, Nordic Heritage
photos.alt: A gabled grey granite runestone stands in a green late-summer meadow, its face covered by an interlaced serpent design painted deep red, with leafy trees behind it.
photos.caption: Attributed to the carver Livsten, its whole face is filled by a single looping serpent in red.

photos.title: The Sigurd Carving at Ramsund
photos.theme: Rock Carving, Runic Inscription, Viking Age, Norse Saga, Nordic Heritage
photos.alt: Faint red-painted lines on a large sloping grey rock form the Sigurd Carving — a curving runic band wrapped around figures, a dragon, and scenes from the Norse saga — at the edge of a sunlit summer woodland.
photos.caption: An eleventh-century rock carving retelling the saga of Sigurd the dragon-slayer, its runic band recording a bridge raised across the Ramsund channel.

photos.title: Detail of the Sigurd Carving at Ramsund
photos.theme: Rock Carving, Runic Inscription, Viking Age, Norse Saga, Nordic Heritage
photos.alt: A close detail of the Sigurd Carving: red-painted runes run along a curving band — the dragon's body — beside looping carved figures and a human hand, incised into weathered grey rock flecked with lichen.
photos.caption: The dragon's body carries the runes — the memorial text recording a bridge raised across the Ramsund channel.

photos.title: Figures of the Sigurd Carving at Ramsund
photos.theme: Rock Carving, Runic Inscription, Viking Age, Norse Saga, Nordic Heritage
photos.alt: A close detail of the Sigurd Carving showing red-painted figures — interlacing beasts and a bird — above a band of runes, all incised into cracked, lichen-flecked grey rock.
photos.caption: Saga figures in red: beasts and a bird from the Sigurd legend, threaded above the runic band.

photos.title: Grani and the Runic Band, Sigurd Carving
photos.theme: Rock Carving, Runic Inscription, Viking Age, Norse Saga, Nordic Heritage
photos.alt: A close detail of the Sigurd Carving showing red-painted figures — a laden horse and birds among looping lines — set above a long band of runes incised into the sloping grey rock.
photos.caption: The horse Grani and the saga's birds, carved above the running line of runes.

photos.title: Kärnan Tower in Helsingborg
photos.theme: Castle, Medieval Keep, Brick Architecture, Nordic Heritage
photos.alt: The tall square brick keep of Kärnan rises from a grassy mound in Helsingborg, its crenellated top flying a Swedish flag against a bright blue spring sky, with a wooden stair climbing to its arched doorway.
photos.caption: Helsingborg's medieval keep, completed around 1320 and still the city's landmark above the Sound.

photos.title: At the Foot of Kärnan, Helsingborg
photos.theme: Castle, Medieval Keep, Street Scene, Nordic Heritage
photos.alt: Visitors stand and climb the wide stone terrace steps leading up to the tall brick keep of Kärnan in Helsingborg, which flies a blue-and-yellow flag against a bright blue spring sky.
photos.caption: Visitors gather on the terrace steps below Helsingborg's medieval keep.

photos.title: Climbing the Terrace Steps to Kärnan
photos.theme: Street, Stairs, Light and Shadow, Old Town
photos.alt: Seen from above, two people climb a broad stone staircase toward Kärnan in Helsingborg, their long shadows falling across the steps beside a fan-patterned cobblestone landing in bright spring light.
photos.caption: Hard spring light on the terrace steps that climb to Helsingborg's old castle hill.

photos.title: The Terrace Staircase Below Kärnan
photos.alt: A monumental brick-and-stone terrace staircase rises from a cobbled Helsingborg square toward the twin round towers and the Kärnan keep above, with a bronze statue and a few people climbing the steps in bright spring light.
photos.caption: The grand Terrace Staircase climbing from the old town up to Kärnan's hill.
